description: Missing strict_types lets PHP coerce a wrong-typed value, so a bug surfaces as corrupted data far from its origin.
body: Failure mode: a file without declare(strict_types=1) passes a numeric string or null into a typed parameter and PHP silently coerces it (''→0, '5'→5, null→0), so a type mistake produces wrong values instead of an error and the bug appears far downstream. Detection signals: a value arrives as the wrong type but no error fires; loose == comparisons treat '0' and false alike; the file lacks strict_types. Do not depend on weak-mode coercion for correctness. Safer fallback: add declare(strict_types=1) and precise type hints at boundaries, use === for comparisons that must not coerce, and test that a wrong-typed argument throws a TypeError.
